scenarios.scenario: Prospect says YES — wants the embed/widget
scenarios.response: Immediately send the embed code with simple instructions. Offer to help with sizing or placement. NO anchor text suggestions unless they ask. End with: 'Let me know if you need a short description for the tool — happy to provide one.'
scenarios.anchor_pressure: NONE — do not suggest anchor text even if they embed
scenarios.notes: The embed code itself includes a subtle 'Powered by QFINHUB' link. That's sufficient.

scenarios.scenario: Prospect says YES — wants a custom example
scenarios.response: Ask clarifying questions: what specific calculation, what variables should readers adjust, any specific formatting needs. Build and deliver within 48 hours. Follow up to confirm it works.
scenarios.notes: Custom examples earn natural links when the publisher credits the source. Don't push.

scenarios.scenario: Prospect asks 'how much does it cost' or implies payment expected
scenarios.response: Clarify it's completely free with no catch. If they explicitly ask for payment in exchange for placement: LOG AS PAID INQUIRY. Politely decline unless it's clearly marked as sponsored/nofollow per Google guidelines. Do NOT pay for dofollow links.
scenarios.notes: Paid links = toxic. Reject all paid dofollow requests.

scenarios.scenario: Prospect asks for reciprocal link exchange
scenarios.response: Hold for review. Do not agree immediately. Link exchanges are low-value and risk penalty. Respond: 'I appreciate the offer — let me review and get back to you.' Escalate to manual decision.
scenarios.notes: Reciprocal links have near-zero SEO value in 2026. Only accept if the other site is genuinely high-quality and the link is editorially relevant.

scenarios.scenario: Prospect wants customization (specific parameters, colors, sizing)
scenarios.response: Accommodate simple requests (sizing, default values). For complex requests: offer to create a custom version. Delight them — custom work builds relationships that lead to natural links.
scenarios.notes: Customization = stickiness. A publisher who invests time customizing is more likely to link.

scenarios.response: Send ONE short follow-up: 'Hi [Name], just checking if the [widget/calculator] would be useful for your readers. No worries if not — let me know either way!' Do NOT send a third email.
scenarios.notes: Maximum 2 touches per prospect. Respect the silence.

scenarios.scenario: Prospect replies with 'not interested' or 'we don't accept external tools'
scenarios.response: Thank them for responding. Do not argue or pitch again. Move on.
scenarios.notes: A 'no' is a gift — saves you time. Log and move on.

scenarios.scenario: Prospect embeds the widget but doesn't link or uses nofollow
scenarios.response: Do NOT complain or ask for dofollow. The embed is free. The 'Powered by QFINHUB' link provides some value. A nofollow link from a real site still drives traffic and brand awareness.
scenarios.anchor_pressure: ZERO — do not request anchor changes
scenarios.notes: Nofollow links have indirect SEO value (traffic, brand signals). Don't be greedy.

scenarios.scenario: Prospect replies positively but doesn't follow through (embeds or links)
scenarios.response: One gentle follow-up after 10-14 days: 'Hi [Name], just checking if you had a chance to add the calculator — happy to help if you ran into any issues!' Then stop.
scenarios.notes: Some publishers mean well but get busy. One reminder, then move on.

golden_rules: NEVER ask for specific anchor text
NEVER mention SEO, backlinks, or link building
NEVER send more than 3 emails to a single prospect
ALWAYS lead with reader value, not QFINHUB value
ALWAYS log every interaction (reply, embed, link, rejection)
ALWAYS be genuinely helpful — the link is a side effect
